content: Just as the medial prefrontal cortex modulates and gates hippocampal memory retrieval to suppress irrelevant associations and prioritize target memory recall in the human brain, artificial memory networks can incorporate a prefrontal-inspired gating controller that regulates long-term memory access to stop retrieval of outdated, irrelevant memories and improve downstream inference accuracy. This cross-domain adaptation can address the common problem of noisy, interfering memory retrieval in large-scale memory networks.
